title: Talking (And Singing) Of The Nordic Man
content:
I

    Behold, my child, the Nordic man,
    And be as like him, as you can;
    His legs are long, his mind is slow,
    His hair is lank and made of tow.


II

    And here we have the Alpine Race:
    Oh! What a broad and foolish face!
    His skin is of a dirty yellow.
    He is a most unpleasant fellow.


III

    The most degraded of them all
    Mediterranean we call.
    His hair is crisp, and even curls,
    And he is saucy with the girls.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Hilaire Belloc delivers a powerful performance in "Talking (And Singing) Of The Nordic Man"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
